notes: Day-by-day reveal schedule. The game opens narrow — Graustark, four agents, one problem type, no Dean — and unfolds its mechanics over the first three weeks. Each reveal fires its log line on the day it lands; in phase 2 the same reveals will write into BBS threads. The reveal pacing is *spec'd*, not procedural; this file is the authority and the engine reads it once at scenario start.

reveals.log: Day 3 · [b]Filly[/b] checks in from the bayou. She's still fast. She still doesn't sit still.
Day 4 · The river is doing what the river does. [b]Weir[/b] is in the bayou the way Weir is in the bayou.
Day 5 · The numbers in the corner of the page started being numbers you watched. Insight. Cover. Courier capacity.
Day 6 · The cathedral's variance is up for review. The storefront's beer-and-wine renewal is on the desk. Lease problems are on the board now.
Day 7 · Sunday. [b]Steamboat[/b] is in the canal at the storefront, slow and old. He moves the way he moves.
Day 8 · [b]Harmony Creek Estate.[/b] Mackenzie writes from the model home: the HOA is meeting Tuesdays now. The estate is on the map.
Day 8 · [b]Mackenzie[/b] is in Phase 3. The loom is, this week, in the second bedroom.
Day 10 · [b]Cicada[/b] has been at the cul-de-sac since 2016. You'd forgotten. She had not.
Day 11 · The starlings rose over Phase 3 in a recognisable shape. [b]Starling[/b] is on dispatch.
Day 12 · Harmony Creek's problem palette opens up. Watch the newsletter.
Day 12 · The demons' [b]burn[/b] is starting to matter. The dossier rows now read in numbers, not silhouettes.
Day 14 · [b]Elicia[/b] writes from the bungalow archive: she'd like to be useful. The choose-your-own-adventure episode is forty months overdue.
Day 15 · [b]The Surviving D'Ambrosio Son[/b] checks in. The storefront's Friday dinner held; he can dispatch.
Day 18 · A letter from [b]Jules[/b] at the timber yard. The room above the office is still here. [b]Small Wood, Oregon[/b] is on the map. Watch the cost.
Day 18 · [b]Jules[/b] is the only person Frasier knows in Small Wood. He's the budget you spend there.
Day 19 · The human roster's [b]obligation[/b] column starts reading. Each favour costs something. The fifth favour is harder than the second.

Day 21 · [color=#a8a8c0]Aria, fourteen, writes a sentence about a plain-faced man at the diner. The next morning, on the Small Wood map: there is a tower. You did not, before, know there was a tower.[/color]
Day 22 · Your first demon comes back from Small Wood. The [b]corruption[/b] column on the dossier is no longer hidden. It reads in numbers now.
Day 25 · Small Wood's problem palette opens up. Press, contacts, the ground itself.
Day 28 · Your demons have done four weeks of work for you. Steamboat has started posting on RUST_CODE under his own handle. The [b]complexity[/b] column is now visible.
Day 31 · The first July storm. Graustark's full problem palette opens up. The Sinkhole anniversary is mid-July.
Day 42 · A demon you sent to Small Wood comes back slightly different. [b]Demon corruption events[/b] are now on the table.
Day 50 · Halfway. [b]Husk[/b] is reachable now if the summer asks for him. You have used him twice in twenty years. The third use is a saga-ender.
Day 60 · The interlude shelf in the corner has started getting [color=#c8a8ff]Dean[/color] entries. They are sparse and unreliable. You cannot read his ledger; you can read what he leaves.
Day 75 · Late August. The riverboat anniversary is in nine days. Graustark's grief-problems will tick faster through Labor Day.
Day 90 · Ten days to Labor Day. The shelf is what it is. The seeds are what they are.

Day 66 · Ruth lays three documents side by side on the diner table: a buyout letter, the charter review notice, the census stranger's lease. Same notary. Same font. Same building. [b]The County Seat[/b] is on the map — and for the first time all summer, the problems are theirs.
Day 66 · The co-op opens a folder of its own. Records requests. Deadlines that are THEIR deadlines. The palette is offense now, when you choose it.
